name: Roman Pantheon
overview: The Pantheon is a former Roman temple and now a Catholic church, renowned for its remarkable architecture and enormous unsupported concrete dome. It's one of the best-preserved Roman buildings and a testament to ancient Roman engineering.
history: Originally built by Marcus Agrippa in 27 BC, the current structure was rebuilt by Emperor Hadrian around 126 AD. It has been in continuous use throughout its history, first as a Roman temple to all gods, then as a Christian church since 609 AD.
highlights: Massive concrete dome with central oculus (opening)
Perfect proportions - height equals diameter (43.3 meters)
Raphael's tomb and other notable burials
Ancient Roman marble columns and floors
tips: Entry is free but expect crowds during peak hours. The oculus provides natural lighting - visit during rain to see water fall through the opening. Best photography light is midday when sun shines through the oculus.
